edges.reason: Smaller grains increase boundary area per unit volume geometrically.
Dislocation stored energy provides thermodynamic driving force for recrystallization.
Recrystallization nucleates new grains changing size distribution.
Recrystallized grains are dislocation-free, reducing overall dislocation density.
Low stacking fault energy inhibits dislocation cross-slip and recovery.
Precipitates exert Zener pinning pressure restricting grain boundary migration.
Strong texture constrains grain orientations, narrowing misorientation distribution.
Grain elongation from deformation strengthens crystallographic texture alignment.
Grain size influences boundary character through grain growth kinetics.
Precipitates pin boundaries, inhibiting recrystallization nucleation and growth.
High boundary density provides more nucleation sites for recrystallization.
Elongated grains result from deformation which increases dislocation density.

cycles_resolved.resolution: Temporal separation: dislocation accumulation precedes recrystallization which then reduces dislocations.
cycles_resolved.method: Dynamic process stages prevent logical cycle in steady-state DAG.

cycles_resolved.resolution: Removed d7->d9 or accept temporal dynamics where recrystallization evolves microstructure.
cycles_resolved.method: Retained d7->d9 as nucleation site effect is distinct mechanism.

causal_graph.notes: DAG represents steady-state causal structure. Temporal cycles resolved through process staging.
